title: What happens to your PPF account if you move abroad? NRI rules explained
summary: NRIs can keep their PPF accounts active, but cannot open new ones. They must maintain a minimum deposit and cannot extend the tenure beyond 15 years. Here's how the proceeds are handled, among other rules you should be aware of.

title: FD at PSU banks vs private banks vs SFBs – How much can ₹5 lakh earn in 5 years? Check best rates here
summary: Bank of Baroda offers the highest 5-year FD rate among PSU banks at 6.30%. DCB Bank leads private banks with 7.25%, while Suryoday Small Finance Bank tops SFBs at 7.90%. Fixed deposits remain popular for their predictability and safety.

title: DA hike calculator: When is next Dearness allowance hike? How much to expect? How it will impact your salary
summary: On April 18, the Centre raised the dearness allowance for employees by 2%, increasing it to 60%. The next DA hike is expected in July 2026, with a potential increase of 2-3% depending on inflation trends, benefiting approximately 50.46 lakh employees and 68.27 lakh pensioners.

title: Wealthy families are turning to AI-driven investment and strategy advisory firms - Here's why

title: I asked ChatGPT to plan an international family holiday in June — these destinations made the list
summary: A budget-friendly 10-day international family trip from Mumbai can include destinations such as Thailand, Vietnam, Bali, Malaysia, and Sri Lanka, all within a budget of ₹2–3 lakh.&nbsp;

title: EPFO 3.0 update: PF withdrawal via ATM, UPI soon – Check the expected launch date
summary: EPFO subscribers will soon be able to withdraw funds via ATMs, enhancing convenience. This change, part of the EPFO 3.0 initiative, is expected to be implemented by the end of May, allowing quick access to provident funds with minimal paperwork.

title: Quant MF’s Sandeep Tandon says this is India’s biggest buying opportunity since covid
summary: Quant Mutual Fund founder and CIO Sandeep Tandon says fear around crude, the rupee and FII exits has created opportunity in undervalued pockets of the market, even as investors crowd into consensus trades.

title: Building an emergency fund? Here's where you can invest for quick access to funds and optimal returns
summary: An emergency fund should be easily accessible and low-risk. You will have to allocate your funds such that they can be liquidated for immediate needs while giving optimal returns without compromising safety.

summary: The revised NPS was launched on the lines of the Centre's unified pension scheme (UPS), a default pension scheme rolled out for the new recruits joining central government jobs starting 1 April 2025.
